claim: Deamination of cytidine to uridine on the minus strand of viral DNA results in catastrophic G-to-A mutations in the viral genome.

result.claim: Deamination of cytidine to uridine on the minus strand of viral DNA results in catastrophic G-to-A mutations in the viral genome.
result.reasoning: The claim is strongly supported by evidence from paper 4347374. This paper states that "APOBEC3G exerts its antiviral effect during reverse transcription to trigger G-to-A hypermutation in the nascent retroviral DNA" (prop_1617). The abstract of paper 4347374 further explains that APOBEC family members, including APOBEC3G, have "potent DNA mutator activity through dC deamination." Deamination of deoxycytidine (dC) to deoxyuridine (dU) on the minus strand of viral DNA during reverse transcription would lead to the incorporation of adenine (A) opposite the deoxyuridine during the synthesis of the plus strand, ultimately resulting in G-to-A mutations in the viral genome. While the term "catastrophic" is not explicitly used, "hypermutation" implies a significant and detrimental level of mutation, consistent with the spirit of the claim. The mechanism described in the paper directly aligns with the claim's assertion of cytidine deamination on the minus strand of viral DNA leading to G-to-A mutations.

evidence.text: N348I was associated with non-nucleoside RTI resistance mutations K103N and Y181C/I.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: APOBEC3G exerts its antiviral effect during reverse transcription to trigger G-to-A hypermutation in the nascent retroviral DNA.
evidence.source: Broad antiretroviral defence by human APOBEC3G through lethal editing of nascent reverse transcripts

evidence.text: Genetic mutations in TAR DNA-binding protein 43 cause amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.
evidence.source: The Inhibition of TDP-43 Mitochondrial Localization Blocks Its Neuronal Toxicity

evidence.text: Damage-induced fork reversal in mammalian cells requires PCNA ubiquitination.
evidence.source: Replication Fork Slowing and Reversal upon DNA Damage Require PCNA Polyubiquitination and ZRANB3 DNA Translocase Activity

evidence.text: Damage-induced fork reversal requires K63-linked polyubiquitin chains.
evidence.source: Replication Fork Slowing and Reversal upon DNA Damage Require PCNA Polyubiquitination and ZRANB3 DNA Translocase Activity

evidence.text: Clinically relevant doses of DNA methyltransferase and histone deacetylase inhibitors (DNMTi and HDACi) reduce the immune suppressive microenvironment through type I IFN signaling.
evidence.source: Epigenetic therapy activates type I interferon signaling in murine ovarian cancer to reduce immunosuppression and tumor burden.

evidence.text: Fork reversal is driven in vitro by multiple enzymes, including the DNA translocase ZRANB3.
evidence.source: Replication Fork Slowing and Reversal upon DNA Damage Require PCNA Polyubiquitination and ZRANB3 DNA Translocase Activity

evidence.text: The mutations result in enhanced suppression of p53 in response to ionizing radiation exposure.
evidence.source: Mosaic PPM1D mutations are associated with predisposition to breast and ovarian cancer

evidence.text: Each strand of the CoYMV genome is interrupted by site-specific discontinuities.
evidence.source: Properties of Commelina yellow mottle virus's complete DNA sequence, genomic discontinuities and transcript suggest that it is a pararetrovirus.

evidence.text: N348I was highly associated with thymidine analogue mutations M41L and T215Y/F.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: Mutations were identified in the NT5C2 gene using whole-exome sequencing.
evidence.source: Activating mutations in the NT5C2 nucleotidase gene drive chemotherapy resistance in relapsed ALL

evidence.text: Mutations affecting fork reversal also induced unrestrained fork progression and chromosomal breakage.
evidence.source: Replication Fork Slowing and Reversal upon DNA Damage Require PCNA Polyubiquitination and ZRANB3 DNA Translocase Activity

evidence.text: N348I was associated with lamivudine resistance mutations M184V/I.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: Non-shared mutations are primarily G-to-T, a signature associated with oxidative stress.
evidence.source: Evolution of metastasis revealed by mutational landscapes of chemically induced skin cancers

evidence.text: Almost all known RT inhibitor resistance mutations identified to date map to the polymerase domain of the enzyme.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: APOBEC3G can act on a broad range of retroviruses in addition to HIV.
evidence.source: Broad antiretroviral defence by human APOBEC3G through lethal editing of nascent reverse transcripts

evidence.text: Each strand of the CoYMV genome is interrupted by site-specific discontinuities.
evidence.source: Properties of Commelina yellow mottle virus's complete DNA sequence, genomic discontinuities and transcript suggest that it is a pararetrovirus.

evidence.text: DNA damage tolerance during eukaryotic replication is orchestrated by PCNA ubiquitination.
evidence.source: Replication Fork Slowing and Reversal upon DNA Damage Require PCNA Polyubiquitination and ZRANB3 DNA Translocase Activity

evidence.text: Viruses exploit the mechanism of N1-2'O-methyltransferase to escape RIG-I.
evidence.source: A Conserved Histidine in the RNA Sensor RIG-I Controls Immune Tolerance to N1-2'O-Methylated Self RNA.

evidence.text: DNA was genotyped for CYP2D6 variants associated with reduced (*10, *41) or absent (*3, *4, *5) enzyme activity.
evidence.source: Association between CYP2D6 polymorphisms and outcomes among women with early stage breast cancer treated with tamoxifen.

evidence.text: Yellow-fever-virus-encoded 2'O-methyltransferase can be exploited by viruses.
evidence.source: A Conserved Histidine in the RNA Sensor RIG-I Controls Immune Tolerance to N1-2'O-Methylated Self RNA.

evidence.text: NVP-BEZ235 reversed the hyperactivation of the PI3K/mTOR pathway caused by the oncogenic mutations of p110-alpha, E545K, and H1047R.
evidence.source: NVP-BEZ235, a dual PI3K/mTOR inhibitor, prevents PI3K signaling and inhibits the growth of cancer cells with activating PI3K mutations.

evidence.text: PPM1D PTV mutations were present in 25 out of 7,781 cases versus 1 out of 5,861 controls.
evidence.source: Mosaic PPM1D mutations are associated with predisposition to breast and ovarian cancer

evidence.text: Genomes are typically depleted of CG dinucleotides because of imperfect repair of deaminated methylcytosines.
evidence.source: Dnmt1-Independent CG Methylation Contributes to Nucleosome Positioning in Diverse Eukaryotes

evidence.text: Monoubiquitination activates mutagenic translesion synthesis.
evidence.source: Replication Fork Slowing and Reversal upon DNA Damage Require PCNA Polyubiquitination and ZRANB3 DNA Translocase Activity

evidence.text: DNA polymerase mu (pol mu), lambda (pol lambda), and terminal deoxynucleotidyltransferase (TdT) are enzymes of the pol X family.
evidence.source: Nonoverlapping functions of DNA polymerases mu, lambda, and terminal deoxynucleotidyltransferase during immunoglobulin V(D)J recombination in vivo.

evidence.text: Somatic mutations activate phosphoinositide 3-kinase (PI3K) in the p110-alpha catalytic subunit (encoded by PIK3CA).
evidence.source: Effective Use of PI3K and MEK Inhibitors to Treat Mutant K-Ras G12D and PIK3CA H1047R Murine Lung Cancers

evidence.text: When produced in the presence of APOBEC3G, vif-defective virus is non-infectious.
evidence.source: Broad antiretroviral defence by human APOBEC3G through lethal editing of nascent reverse transcripts

evidence.text: Dmc1Δ and dmc1Δ rad51Δ mutants accumulate single-strand DNA (ssDNA)–associated DSBs.
evidence.source: Mapping Meiotic Single-Strand DNA Reveals a New Landscape of DNA Double-Strand Breaks in Saccharomyces cerevisiae

evidence.text: TET family enzymes convert 5-methylcytosine (5mC) to 5-hydroxymethylcytosine (5hmC) in DNA.
evidence.source: Tet1 and Tet2 regulate 5-hydroxymethylcytosine production and cell lineage specification in mouse embryonic stem cells.

evidence.text: The helical domain (E545K and E542K) and the kinase domain (H1047R) are two hotspots where PI3K mutations are most frequently observed.
evidence.source: Effective Use of PI3K and MEK Inhibitors to Treat Mutant K-Ras G12D and PIK3CA H1047R Murine Lung Cancers

evidence.text: Dmc1Δ and dmc1Δ rad51Δ mutants are repair-defective.
evidence.source: Mapping Meiotic Single-Strand DNA Reveals a New Landscape of DNA Double-Strand Breaks in Saccharomyces cerevisiae

evidence.text: N348I decreased zidovudine susceptibility 2- to 4-fold in the context of wildtype HIV-1.
evidence.source: N348I in the Connection Domain of HIV-1 Reverse Transcriptase Confers Zidovudine and Nevirapine Resistance

evidence.text: Ubc4 and Ubc5 are ubiquitin conjugating enzymes (E2) that are involved in the ubiquitylation of excess histones.
evidence.source: Histone levels are regulated by phosphorylation and ubiquitylation dependent proteolysis
